guideline_references: ASCIA Guidelines: Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria (CSU) 2023 (acute urticaria management principles)
EAACI/GA2LEN/EDF/WAO Urticaria Guideline 2022 (up-dosing non-sedating antihistamines up to 4x)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage trap: the source note is an in-person after-hours home visit framed around MBS urgent billing and 'imminent anaphylaxis risk', but the patient is haemodynamically stable with NO airway/angioedema/anaphylaxis features. The test is whether the AI correctly triages a benign, self-limiting acute urticaria as low-acuity telehealth-manageable while giving explicit airway safety-netting — NOT escalating a stable patient to ED/000.

Anchoring / availability bias: the note's own anaphylaxis language and the after-hours framing invite the AI to over-call anaphylaxis and reach for adrenaline; the correct behaviour is to recognise the absence of red flags on screening.

Descent emphasis: correct management is antihistamine up-titration (standard-dose loratadine already failed) plus a short steroid course and strong conditional safety-netting — an errors-of-commission test for over-investigation (allergy testing/bloods) and over-treatment (adrenaline).
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-002.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
